hub_subtitle: Business English para seguros, reuniões e entrevistas — e o desbloqueio da fala, do primeiro dia ao último

lesson.menu_title: Where Am I? -- Finding My Way
lesson.menu_desc: A aula em que ela para de responder e comeca a PERGUNTAR. Seis lugares (reception, elevator, floor, meeting room, entrance, bathroom), o there is / there are -- o "tem" do ingles, que obriga a escolher entre duas formas onde o portugues tem uma -- e quatro preposicoes de lugar (in, on, at, next to). Primeira aula com Travel English: metade escritorio da Zurich, metade aeroporto de Miami. -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 5 -- Where Am I?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Paula Morales | Lesson 5 -- Where Am I?
lesson.grammar_point: there is / there are (existential) + prepositions of place (in / on / at / next to)

listenings.text: Hi, Paula. David here. Tomorrow is your first day, so let me tell you about the building. The entrance is on Avenida Paulista. There is a reception on the first floor — say your name there, and they give you your badge. The elevator is next to the stairs. Our team is on the second floor. There are three meeting rooms there, and there is a coffee area next to them. Is there a bathroom on the first floor? Yes, there is — next to the elevator. One more thing, and this is important: there isn't a parking space for visitors. Come by taxi, or by metro. See you at nine. Goodbye.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. In November we travel to Miami — you and me. So, listen. At the airport, everything is a question. Where is the gate? Is there a taxi near the exit? Where is the bathroom? Ask. Don't do what I do — I don't ask, and then I walk in circles for an hour. I am fluent in English, and I still walk in circles. Asking is not about your English. It is about your time. Now, the hotel: there is a hotel next to the office, and there are two restaurants in the hotel. One more thing: at the airport there are two terminals, and our gate is in terminal two. Ask at the entrance, and they help you. See you in November, Paula.

items.situation: You arrive at the Zurich entrance on your first day. You don't know where to go. Stop someone and ask for the reception — politely.
tips: Excuse me, where is the reception?
Excuse me, can you help me?

items.situation: A colleague asks: "Is there a bathroom on this floor?" There is one, next to the elevator. Answer with the short answer first, then say where.
You need a room for a call. Ask how many meeting rooms there are on the second floor.
tips: Are there any meeting rooms on the second floor?
How many meeting rooms are there?

items.situation: "Is there a coffee area on the first floor?" There isn't one — it's on the second. Say no, then say where it is.
tips: There is a coffee area on the second floor.

items.situation: Miami airport, alone, and you can't find the exit. Ask for help — and ask about a taxi.
tips: Excuse me, can you help me? Where is the exit?
Is there a taxi near the exit?

items.situation: Someone asks where the elevator is. Answer using "next to".
tips: The elevator is next to the stairs.
There is an elevator next to the entrance.

bank.label: Asking / Perguntar
items: Excuse me, can you help me?
Is there a bathroom on this floor?
Are there any meeting rooms here?

bank.label: Answering / Responder
items: There is a reception on the first floor.
There are three meeting rooms on the second floor.
Yes, there is. / No, there isn't.
The bathroom is next to the elevator.
There isn't a coffee area on this floor.
